enriched_points.expanded_content: Conducting polymers (CPs) are organic polymers that conduct electricity, examples include polyaniline, polypyrrole, and poly(3,4-ethylenedioxythiophene) (PEDOT) (Source 1).
CPs exhibit tunable electrical, optical, and mechanical properties, making them ideal for applications in sensors, transistors, and flexible electronics (Source 3, Source 4).

Various techniques like chemical vapor deposition, electrochemical polymerization, and solution casting are used to synthesize and process conducting polymers for specific applications (Source 2, Source 5).
